experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z near the top in horizontal orientation. The stack is uneven but not threatening yet; there is a useful vertical notch around columns 4-5 above the existing right-side structure. Goal is to place the Z cleanly without creating overhangs and set up for upcoming T/I/J/O. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would lock the horizontal Z high on the left-middle surface, likely less flexible. Moving left/right first could aim for a better fit, but with one action now the most productive immediate step is to rotate into vertical orientation, which can then be dropped into narrower gaps on subsequent turns. Soft drop is less committal but doesn't improve fit as much as rotation. So rotate right to access the better placement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a horizontal I near the top spanning columns 3-6, and rotating at the current position is blocked. The stack below is uneven with a notable deep left-side channel around columns 0-2, while the center already has mixed blocks. Goal is to place the I to improve future line-clear potential without forcing a bad center lock. Since rotation is unavailable here, the best immediate sub-goal is to move the I toward the left so it can potentially drop into the left channel as a vertical or useful horizontal placement on later turns. Candidate actions: right moves toward more cluttered center-right; soft/hard drop would commit the current horizontal I high on the stack; no_op wastes a turn. Left is the best setup move now.
